name: ship-check-hygiene
description: Use when release readiness depends on legacy removal, document freshness, and public-surface sync. Do not use for branch mutation or rollout-risk judgement.
purpose: Verify release hygiene gates such as required docs, stale or legacy surface removal, and registry-to-doc sync before release judgement or publication.
when_to_use: Need to confirm required release docs are upgraded before shipping.
Need to check that legacy files, stale docs, or removed public skills are actually cleaned up.
Need explicit evidence that registry, user docs, and public surface still agree.
do_not_use: Need git branch, tag, or remote state only; use ship-check-repo instead.
Need rollout risk or rollback judgement; use ship-go-nogo instead.
Need to rewrite docs directly; use document, doc-curate, or doc-publish-readme instead.

required_inputs.name: REQUIRED_DOCS
required_inputs.description: Docs that must be updated or present before release.

required_inputs.name: SURFACE_CONTRACTS
required_inputs.description: Public-surface contracts such as registry/docs parity or required path ownership.

required_inputs.name: LEGACY_PATTERNS
required_inputs.description: Names, files, or patterns that must not survive the release.

input_contract_notes: REQUIRED_DOCS should list only release-blocking docs, not every nice-to-have document in the repository.
SURFACE_CONTRACTS should be observable contracts, such as registry/docs parity or root README ownership, not abstract taste rules.
LEGACY_PATTERNS is for concrete legacy removal checks, not for broad cleanup brainstorming.
Prefer exact skill names, file paths, or anchored patterns over broad words such as 'release' that would match unrelated content.

structured_outputs.name: HYGIENE_FINDINGS
structured_outputs.description: Concrete hygiene problems such as stale docs, leftover legacy surface, or unsynced public references.

structured_outputs.name: DOC_GATE_STATUS
structured_outputs.description: Whether required release docs are ready.

structured_outputs.name: SURFACE_SYNC_STATUS
structured_outputs.description: Whether registry, skill docs, and user-facing docs stay in sync.

structured_outputs.name: REQUIRED_CLEANUPS
structured_outputs.description: Bounded cleanup actions required before release.

output_contract_notes: If required release docs are missing or stale, DOC_GATE_STATUS should be blocked instead of buried in a note.
SURFACE_SYNC_STATUS should reflect observable mismatches only, such as registry/doc drift or surviving legacy names.
REQUIRED_CLEANUPS should stay bounded and release-relevant rather than turning into a generic improvement backlog.
HYGIENE_FINDINGS and REQUIRED_CLEANUPS may both be empty when docs and public surface are already clean.
neutrality_rules: Do not invent hygiene blockers when docs and public surface evidence is clean.
Treat missing release docs or stale public references as release risk, not as optional polish.
Keep release-blocking hygiene issues separate from non-blocking cleanup ideas.
execution_constraints: Do not edit files from this skill; it is a check-only release gate.
If docs upgrade is required and not evidenced, block release instead of assuming the docs will catch up later.
Keep findings traceable to concrete files, paths, or public-surface contracts.
default_program:
[stages: preflight>detect>analyze>review>handoff>audit |
 scope: diff|repo |
 policy: evidence,safety-gates,quality-gates{docs,release,registry},deterministic-output |
 lens: release-gatekeeper |
 output: md(contract=v1)]
lens_rationale: Release hygiene should be judged as delivery safety, not as optional cleanup, because stale docs and legacy public surface create real rollout risk.
